title: Baby Changing Table with Drawers and Topper
summary: A two-shelf changing table on 2x2 legs with two built-in drawers below and a raised-edge topper on top to keep a changing pad from sliding off.
description:
The upper shelf height is genuinely yours to choose — this plan places it to leave 12" of clearance on the lower shelf, but the pocket-hole leg construction doesn't care where that shelf sits, so measure your own storage bins or baskets first and set the height to fit them. Drill every pocket hole facing the direction it needs before assembly: upward on the aprons and 1x6 sides for attaching the top later, and outward on the drawer-area trim for attaching to the legs — going back to add a pocket hole after the case is together is much harder than planning ahead.

Build the two drawers to fit their actual openings and the 14" slides you're using, inset 3/4" from the front face to leave room for the drawer faces. The topper is a separate, optional assembly with no need for a bottom — it can be screwed down or simply held in place with a bracket on the back, since its job is just to keep a changing pad from sliding off the table, not to bear weight.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four legs at 32" from the Pine 2x2. Cut four side trim at 14-1/2", four front and back trim at 38", two drawer area trim, long at 38" and six drawer area trim, short at 4-3/4" from the Pine 1x2. Cut two shelves at 38" from the 3/4" plywood or MDF, half sheet. Cut two sides (aprons) at 14-1/2" and one back (apron) at 38" from the Pine 1x6. Cut one top at 41" from the 3/4" plywood or MDF, half sheet. Cut four drawer sides at 15-1/2" and four drawer front/back at 15-3/8" from the Pine 1x4. Cut two drawer bottoms at 16-7/8" from the 1/4" plywood, at least 16" x 35". Cut two drawer faces at 17-5/8" from the Pine 1x6. Cut two topper long sides at 41" and three topper ends at 16" from the Pine 1x3. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to two 8 ft Pine 2x2, four 8 ft Pine 1x2, one 10 ft Pine 1x6, one 12 ft Pine 1x4, one 10 ft Pine 1x6 and two 8 ft Pine 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Attach the side trim to the legs
steps.body: Drill two 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the 14-1/2" side trim pieces. Attach to the legs with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, flush to the inside, keeping pocket holes hidden. The upper shelf can sit at any height — this plan leaves 12" of clearance under the lower shelf.

steps.title: Attach the front and back trim
steps.body: Drill two 3/4" pocket holes on each end of the 38" trim pieces and attach to the legs with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, at the same heights as the side trim, flush to the inside of the legs.

steps.title: Attach the shelves
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes around all four edges of both shelves, spacing them every 6-8". Attach inset to the trim frames with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the side and back aprons
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes facing upward on the 1x6 boards for attaching the top later, and 3/4" pocket holes on the ends for attaching to the legs. Attach the side and back aprons with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, keeping the back apron flush to the outside of the legs.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top through the predrilled holes in the 1x6 aprons with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Build the drawer-area frames
steps.body: Attach the shorter drawer-area trim to the inside of the drawer opening. Attach the long bottom trim piece with glue and finish nails, also using pocket holes to attach to the legs. Repeat for the second frame, positioning it so the 14" drawer slides can be attached at the back to the shorter trim pieces.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build each drawer to fit its opening and the drawer slides, using 1-1/4" brad nails and glue for the box and 3/4" brad nails and glue for the bottom. Install with the drawer slides, insetting 3/4" to allow room for the drawer face.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces
steps.body: Attach each drawer face with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue, then reinforce from inside with 1-1/4" screws. It's easier to attach handles or knobs before attaching the faces.

steps.title: Build and attach the topper
steps.body: Build the topper frame with finish nails and glue or pocket holes — no bottom is needed. Attach it to the top of the table, or secure with a bracket on the back to keep it in place.

images.alt: A baby changing table with two drawers and a raised-edge topper
